oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature10_1_missing_block.json.description: Invoking the distribution entry point without a block raises a missing-block error.

feature10_2_unknown_keywords.json.description: Invoking the distribution entry point with options it does not recognize raises an unknown-keywords error listing the offending keys in order.

feature1_default_routing.json.description: With no distribution scope active and default mode off, a read is served by the primary database.

feature2_1_block_reads_replica.json.description: A read executed outside any scope uses the primary; inside a distribution scope reads use the replica, and a write performed inside the scope does not pin later reads back to the primary.

feature2_2_transaction_primary.json.description: A read wrapped in a transaction inside a distribution scope is served by the primary, because transactional work must stay on the writable node.

feature3_1_by_default_sticky.json.description: When default-distribution mode is on, a plain read uses the replica; after a write, subsequent reads are pinned to the primary for consistency.

feature3_2_block_inside_default.json.description: With default-distribution mode on, reads inside a distribution scope stay on the replica even after a write within the scope; once the scope exits, an earlier write pins the next read to the primary.

feature3_3_primary_override.json.description: With default-distribution mode on, opening a scope that forces the primary routes its reads back to the primary.

feature4_1_max_lag_exceeded.json.description: When a maximum replica-lag threshold is set and the measured lag exceeds it, opening the scope raises a lag-exceeded error before any read runs. The threshold may be supplied per scope or via global defaults.

feature4_2_max_lag_under.json.description: When a maximum replica-lag threshold is set and the measured lag is at or below it (whether stubbed low or measured live), reads proceed on the replica.

feature4_3_lag_unknown.json.description: When the replica lag cannot be determined (unknown/nil) and a lag threshold is set, opening the scope raises a lag-exceeded error naming the connection.

cases.expected_output:
error=too_much_lag
message=Replica lag is nil on default

feature4_4_lag_failover.json.description: When lag failover is enabled, exceeding the lag threshold (or being unable to measure lag) does not raise; instead a message is logged and reads fall back to the primary.

cases.expected_output:
log=Replica lag over 1 seconds. Falling back to master pool.
read=primary

log=Replica lag is nil on default. Falling back to master pool.
read=primary

feature4_5_lag_on_connection.json.description: When the lag threshold is checked against a named connection (supplied singly or as a list) and that connection's lag exceeds the threshold, the raised error names that connection.

cases.expected_output:
error=too_much_lag
message=Replica lag over 1 seconds on User connection

feature5_replication_lag.json.description: The current replica replication lag can be read as a numeric value.

feature6_1_failover_to_primary.json.description: When no replicas are available, a distribution scope logs the situation and falls back to the primary. This holds whether or not default-distribution mode is on.

cases.expected_output:
log=No replicas available. Falling back to master pool.
read=primary

feature6_2_failover_disabled.json.description: When no replicas are available and failover is disabled, opening the scope raises a no-replicas-available error. This holds whether or not default-distribution mode is on.

feature7_1_force_replica.json.description: Forcing replica use for a scope routes its reads to the replica even when default-distribution mode is off; reads outside the scope still use the primary. SQL comment prefixes do not change routing.

feature7_2_force_replica_failover.json.description: Forcing replica use when no replicas are available logs the situation and falls back to the primary.

cases.expected_output:
log=No replicas available. Falling back to master pool.
read=primary

feature7_3_force_replica_failover_disabl.description: Forcing replica use when no replicas are available and failover is disabled raises a no-replicas-available error.

feature8_1_job_distribution.json.description: A background job declared to distribute its reads runs its reads on the replica.

feature8_2_job_by_default.json.description: With default-distribution mode on, a background job that both reads and writes runs its read on the replica on each invocation, because job context is reset between runs.

feature9_lazy_relation_warning.json.description: When a distribution scope returns a query object that has not been executed yet, a warning is logged advising the caller to force execution inside the scope.

cases.expected_output: log=Call `to_a` inside block to execute query on replica

_constraints.input_condition: Operation code is 'scope' with 'replica': true option
_constraints.output_rule: The 'body' array inside the scope MUST be executed strictly within this replica context, ignoring any global defaults or parent state.

_constraints.input_condition: Operation code is 'scope' with 'primary': true option
_constraints.output_rule: The 'body' array inside the scope MUST be executed strictly within this primary context, overriding any global default-distribution mode or parent state.

_constraints.input_condition: Operation code is 'transaction' exists anywhere in the input program
_constraints.output_rule: All operations (reads and writes) nested inside the transaction body MUST route to 'primary' regardless of outer scope configuration or global mode.

_constraints.input_condition: Input contains a 'write' operation followed by a 'read' operation outside any distribution scope (or after a scope has closed)
_constraints.output_rule: When default-distribution mode is ON, the write pins subsequent reads to 'primary' until a new scope with a different primary/policy is entered.

_constraints.feature: Routing Logic - Write Non-Pinning Within Scope
_constraints.input_condition: Input contains 'write' followed by 'read' inside the same active distribution scope
_constraints.output_rule: Writes inside a distribution scope do NOT pin subsequent reads to 'primary' within that same scope; reads continue to use the replica (unless scope is explicitly pinned to primary).

_constraints.input_condition: Global configuration does not have 'by_default': true
_constraints.output_rule: Any 'read' operation that is NOT inside a distribution scope MUST route to 'primary'.

_constraints.input_condition: Global configuration has 'by_default': true
_constraints.output_rule: Any 'read' operation that is NOT inside a distribution scope MUST route to 'replica'.

_constraints.input_condition: Input contains an operation with literal code exactly equal to 'scope_without_block'
_constraints.output_rule: Abort execution and output error line 'error=missing_block' followed by 'message=Missing block' (case sensitive).

_constraints.input_condition: Input contains 'options' object with keys not in {max_lag, lag_failover, lag_on, replica, primary, failover} (sorted alphabetically in output)
_constraints.output_rule: Abort execution and output error line 'error=unknown_keywords' followed by 'message=Unknown keywords: <comma separated sorted list>' (case sensitive).

_constraints.input_condition: Input contains 'replication_lag' operation without being nested inside a 'scope' operation
_constraints.output_rule: Abort execution and output error 'error=missing_block'.

_constraints.input_condition: Input contains an operation op with content NOT in {read, write, scope, transaction, replication_lag, 'scope_without_block', job}
_constraints.output_rule: Abort execution (implementation implied to handle as invalid), but specific test coverage not provided for generic unknown ops beyond block check.

_constraints.input_condition: Operation is a successful read
_constraints.output_rule: Print exactly: read=<node>

_constraints.input_condition: Operation raises an error (e.g., missing_block, unknown_keywords, lag exceeded, no replicas)
_constraints.output_rule:
Print exactly: error=<code>
message=<text>

_constraints.input_condition: Lag check exceeds threshold and nothing disables failover; no 'lag_on' specified or 'lag_on' is unmet
_constraints.output_rule: Message MUST be exactly:
exact_values: Replica lag is nil on default

_constraints.input_condition: Lag check exceeds threshold via 'lag_on' connection name(s)
_constraints.output_rule: Message MUST append ' on <connection_name>' at the end.

_constraints.input_condition: Lag exceeds threshold RAG_FAILOVER is true OR No replicas available AND failover is enabled/default
_constraints.output_rule: Print exactly: log=<message>

_constraints.input_condition: Fall-back routing is triggered
_constraints.output_rule: Message MUST be exactly:

_constraints.input_condition: Operation is 'job' with role 'read_only' OR (default mode ON AND role 'read_write')
_constraints.output_rule: Print exactly: job=<node>

_constraints.input_condition: Operation is 'lazy_read' (no explicit op test, but implied by warning existence)
_constraints.output_rule: Print exactly: log=Call `to_a` inside block to execute query on replica
exact_values: Call `to_a` inside block to execute query on replica

_constraints.input_condition: Input provides 'lag': null
_constraints.output_rule: Treat as exceeding threshold

_constraints.input_condition: Input provides 'failover': false
_constraints.output_rule: Disable graceful fallback; raise error immediately if block starts when logic (replicas unavailable or max lag exceeded) would normally failover.

hide.replacement: a specific error sentinel that triggers an abort
